title: Middle East identity and credential compromise profile
sourceTitle: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2023 Middle East
summary: Middle East breach cost reached SAR 29.9M, with phishing, zero-days, and compromised credentials among leading causes.

title: Global identity and system-intrusion frequency profile
sourceTitle: Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report 2025
summary: Verizon reports ransomware in 44% of breaches globally, third-party involvement doubled, and vulnerability exploitation rose 34%.

title: Middle East data breach cost profile
sourceTitle: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2023 Middle East
summary: Middle East average total breach cost reached SAR 29.9M; lost business, response, and detection were the largest cost drivers.

title: Global data breach profile
sourceTitle: IBM Cost of a Data Breach Report 2025
summary: IBM reports a global average breach cost of USD 4.4M in 2025, with AI security controls reducing cost by about USD 1.9M in high-maturity environments.

title: Global healthcare breach profile
sourceTitle: IBM Healthcare Industry Attack Trends 2024
summary: IBM reports healthcare as the most expensive sector at about USD 9.77M average breach cost.

title: Global financial-services breach profile
sourceTitle: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024: Financial industry
summary: IBM reports average breach cost of USD 6.08M for financial services, second only to healthcare.

title: Global ransomware recovery-cost profile
sourceTitle: Sophos State of Ransomware 2024
summary: Sophos reports average recovery cost of USD 2.73M and average ransom payment of USD 2M among paying organizations.

title: Global ransomware and system-intrusion frequency profile
sourceTitle: Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report 2025
summary: Verizon reports ransomware in 44% of global breaches, 51% in APAC breaches, and median ransom payment around USD 115K.

title: Global cloud and multi-environment breach profile
sourceTitle: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024 multi-environment findings
summary: IBM reports breaches spanning public cloud, private cloud, and on-prem averaged over USD 5M and about 283 days to identify and contain.

title: Global third-party breach profile
sourceTitle: Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report 2025
summary: Verizon reports third-party involvement doubled in 2025, highlighting supplier and partner ecosystem risk.

title: EU supply-chain attack escalation profile
sourceTitle: ENISA Threat Landscape for Supply Chain Attacks 2021
summary: ENISA estimated four times more supply-chain attacks in 2021 than in 2020, with increasing sophistication and APT involvement.

title: EU supplier cybersecurity practice profile
sourceTitle: ENISA Good Practices for Supply Chain Cybersecurity 2023
summary: ENISA documents real-world supplier-risk practices and budget focus areas across essential and important entities in the EU.

title: EU cyber availability and disruption profile
sourceTitle: ENISA Threat Landscape 2024
summary: ENISA identified threats against availability as the top threat category in 2024, followed by ransomware and threats against data.

title: UK organisational cyber breach survey profile
sourceTitle: UK Cyber Security Breaches Survey 2025
summary: UK official statistics report average cost of the most disruptive breach at GBP 1,600 for businesses overall, or GBP 3,550 excluding zero-cost cases.

title: EU supply-chain security maturity profile
sourceTitle: ENISA 2024 Report on the State of Cybersecurity in the Union
summary: ENISA reported 74% of Member States had defined supply-chain security measures in national legislation by 2024.

title: Global industrial-sector breach profile
sourceTitle: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024: Industrial sector
summary: IBM reports average total breach cost of USD 5.56M for industrial organizations, with high sensitivity to downtime and operational disruption.

title: India regional breach profile
sourceTitle: IBM India Cost of a Data Breach 2025
summary: IBM reports average breach cost in India of INR 220M in 2025, with phishing and third-party compromise among the top initial attack vectors.

title: U.S. export-control penalty framework profile
sourceTitle: BIS Enforcement Penalties (2025)
summary: BIS states EAR administrative penalties can reach USD 374,474 per violation or twice the value of the transaction, whichever is greater, with denial of export privileges also possible.

title: Semiconductor export-control enforcement profile
sourceTitle: BIS enforcement actions against Applied Materials, Cadence, and GlobalFoundries
summary: Recent BIS semiconductor-related cases include USD 252M against Applied Materials, USD 95M against Cadence, and USD 500K mitigated against GlobalFoundries, showing how large semiconductor export-control exposure can become.

title: Procurement diligence and sourcing-governance profile
sourceTitle: ISO 20400, OECD due diligence, and global procurement governance guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to procurement governance and due-diligence standards. Emphasis is on weak sourcing decisions, supplier diligence gaps, commercial overpayment, remediation, and regulatory or ESG exposure.

title: Supply chain concentration and dependency profile
sourceTitle: NIST SP 800-161, ISO 28000, and dependency-risk guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to supply chain dependency and resilience guidance. Emphasis is on upstream concentration, substitute-cost pressure, delivery disruption, and contract or resilience impact.

title: Business continuity outage and crisis response profile
sourceTitle: ISO 22301, ISO 22361, and NFPA 1600 continuity guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to continuity, crisis-management, and recovery-governance guidance. Emphasis is on outage duration, management escalation, communications, and recovery slippage.

title: Operational resilience and process-breakdown profile
sourceTitle: Operational resilience and risk-technique guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to operational resilience, risk assessment, and management-response guidance. Emphasis is on process breakdown, service degradation, backlog, and control recovery effort.

title: Financial control breakdown and fraud profile
sourceTitle: COSO Internal Control and BCBS 239 control themes
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to financial-control, data-quality, and reporting-governance guidance. Emphasis is on payment or reporting manipulation, control remediation, and downstream contractual or supervisory exposure.

title: Compliance assurance and anti-bribery control profile
sourceTitle: ISO 37301 and ISO 37001 compliance-system guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to compliance management and anti-bribery control themes. Emphasis is on policy failure, assurance breakdown, investigation cost, remediation, and regulatory response.

title: ESG disclosure, stakeholder, and transition-risk profile
sourceTitle: IFRS S1, GRI, CSRD/ESRS, and UAE sustainability disclosure guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to IFRS-style sustainability disclosure requirements across governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics or targets. Emphasis is on incomplete evidence trails, disclosure remediation, financing pressure, and long-tail reputation impact once reporting cannot be supported.

title: HSE incident, contractor safety, and shutdown profile
sourceTitle: ISO 45001 and regional HSE management-system guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to HSE management-system guidance. Emphasis is on contractor and worker safety, shutdown, remediation, regulatory scrutiny, and management accountability.

title: Business impact analysis and recovery-priority profile
sourceTitle: ISO 22317 and NIST SP 800-34 continuity planning guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for weak business impact analysis, unclear recovery priorities, and poor restoration sequencing. Emphasis is on dependency blindness, slow recovery decisions, and avoidable duration loss.

title: Crisis leadership, communications, and command profile
sourceTitle: ISO 22361, ISO 22320, and NFPA 1600 crisis-management guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for poor crisis command, weak communications, and delayed escalation under pressure. Emphasis is on management confusion, stakeholder trust loss, and prolonged disruption because decision rights fail.

title: Critical dependency and service-restoration profile
sourceTitle: Basel operational resilience, ISO 22301, and service-dependency guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for hidden service dependencies, fragile recovery paths, and restoration failures across critical suppliers or services. Emphasis is on outage amplification once an important dependency fails.

title: Climate disclosure and emissions-control profile
sourceTitle: IFRS S2, TCFD, GHG Protocol, and ISO 14064-1 climate disclosure guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for weak climate disclosures, emissions-data quality problems, and transition-claim challenge. Emphasis is on IFRS-style governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics-or-target failures across physical risk, transition risk, and value-chain emissions reporting.

title: Supply-chain human rights and remediation profile
sourceTitle: OECD RBC, UNGP, CSDDD readiness, and SA8000 labour-assurance guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for supplier labour or rights failures that drive remediation, customer pressure, and disclosure challenge. Emphasis is on due-diligence weakness, corrective action cost, and trust damage across the value chain.

title: Sustainability claims and greenwashing challenge profile
sourceTitle: CSRD/ESRS, ADX ESG guidance, and sustainability-claims governance references
summary: Directional starting profile for overstated sustainability claims, weak evidence trails, and investor or regulator challenge. Emphasis is on narrative overreach, retraction cost, and credibility loss once claims cannot be evidenced.

title: Process safety and loss-of-containment profile
sourceTitle: OSHA PSM, API RP 754, and CCPS process-safety guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for hazardous-process control failure, loss of containment, and emergency shutdown. Emphasis is on major-hazard control weakness, plant disruption, remediation, and regulatory scrutiny.

title: Contractor safety and permit-control profile
sourceTitle: ISO 45001, ILO-OSH, and contractor-control guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for contractor injury, weak permit-to-work discipline, and supervision gaps. Emphasis is on site disruption, investigation effort, and accountable-control weakness rather than isolated unsafe acts.

title: Environmental release and remediation profile
sourceTitle: ISO 14001, Abu Dhabi EHSMS, and environmental-control guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for spills, releases, and environmental-control failure. Emphasis is on clean-up cost, regulatory action, operating interruption, and long-tail stakeholder challenge.

title: AI governance and model-risk profile
sourceTitle: NIST AI RMF and ISO/IEC 42001
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to recognised AI governance references. Emphasis is on weak oversight, unsafe or low-trust outputs, remediation effort, and regulatory or stakeholder challenge rather than pure system outage cost.

title: Data governance and privacy-control profile
sourceTitle: GDPR, UAE PDPL, and enterprise data governance guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for data-governance, privacy, retention, and approved-use failures. Emphasis is on data remediation, regulatory challenge, and reporting-confidence loss once lineage or control breaks down.

title: Fraud, integrity, and financial-crime control profile
sourceTitle: ISO 37001 and UAE AML/CFT control expectations
summary: Directional starting profile for bribery, corruption, collusion, and financial-crime scenarios. Emphasis is on investigation effort, direct loss, legal exposure, and downstream assurance pressure.

title: Legal, contract, and rights-dispute profile
sourceTitle: Contracting, IP, and dispute-governance calibration baseline
summary: Directional starting profile for contract breaches, indemnity disputes, licensing conflicts, and IP-rights challenges. Emphasis is on dispute cost, delayed delivery, and commercial pressure.

title: Geopolitical, sanctions, and market-access profile
sourceTitle: Export-control, sanctions, and market-access restriction guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for sovereign restrictions, sanctions shifts, export-control changes, and market-access disruption. Emphasis is on delayed execution, supplier restriction, and strategic reprioritisation.

title: Physical security and facilities-control profile
sourceTitle: Physical security and executive-protection calibration baseline
summary: Directional starting profile for facility breach, site intrusion, access-control lapse, and executive-protection scenarios. Emphasis is on investigation, operational disruption, and assurance pressure.

title: OT and site-systems resilience profile
sourceTitle: IEC 62443 and site-systems resilience guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for industrial-control instability, degraded telemetry, and OT recovery breakdown. Emphasis is on operating disruption, recovery strain, and safety-linked shutdown pressure.

title: People, workforce, and labour-pressure profile
sourceTitle: UN Guiding Principles, OECD RBC, and worker-welfare guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for workforce pressure, labour-practice escalation, and safe-delivery degradation. Emphasis is on staffing resilience, remediation, and wider confidence or legal consequences.

title: Investment, M&A, and JV execution profile
sourceTitle: Investment and integration-governance calibration baseline
summary: Directional starting profile for investment-theory, integration, and JV-governance downside. Emphasis is on value erosion, delayed synergy, and executive reprioritisation rather than high annual frequency.

title: Transformation and programme-delivery profile
sourceTitle: Programme-delivery and benefits-realisation calibration baseline
summary: Directional starting profile for milestone slippage, delayed go-live, and failed benefit realisation. Emphasis is on delivery drag, rising cost, and value erosion once dependencies slip.

title: United States data breach cost profile
sourceTitle: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2025: United States
summary: IBM reported average United States breach cost of USD 10.22M in 2025, keeping the US well above the global average and reinforcing higher remediation, business disruption, and legal exposure assumptions.

title: United States email-account-compromise and identity-takeover profile
sourceTitle: FBI IC3 2024 Annual Report and business email compromise alerts
summary: IC3 continues to show email account compromise, impersonation, and credential-enabled transfer fraud as major US loss drivers, supporting higher event frequency and response assumptions for mailbox-led takeover scenarios.

title: United States payment-diversion and business-email-compromise profile
sourceTitle: FBI IC3 2024 Annual Report and BEC: The $55 Billion Scam PSA
summary: FBI warnings and IC3 reporting show payment diversion and business email compromise remain major United States loss drivers, with persistent impersonation, invoice manipulation, and wire-transfer redirection patterns.

title: United States outage severity and continuity-loss profile
sourceTitle: Uptime Institute Annual Outage Analysis 2025
summary: Uptime reported that more than half of recent major outages cost over USD 100K and about one in five exceeded USD 1M, reinforcing stronger interruption and crisis-response assumptions for severe continuity events.

title: UAE sustainability disclosure and evidence-trail profile
sourceTitle: UAE sustainability-disclosure principles, IFRS S1/S2 structure, and exchange ESG guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for UAE sustainability reporting that cannot be supported across governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics or targets. Emphasis is on evidence-trail weakness, disclosure remediation, and funding or stakeholder challenge once reporting cannot be reconciled.

title: United States sustainability-claims and greenwashing profile
sourceTitle: FTC Green Guides, SEC ESG enforcement actions, and IFRS-style evidence expectations
summary: Directional starting profile for United States sustainability or climate claims that cannot be substantiated. Emphasis is on weak evidence trails, overstated screening or climate assertions, remediation cost, and enforcement or investor challenge once claims are tested.

title: U.S. compliance retaliation and investigation profile
sourceTitle: U.S. compliance-program and whistleblower-retaliation enforcement guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to whistleblower retaliation, hotline escalation, internal investigations, and code-of-conduct or gifts-and-entertainment control failures. Emphasis is on investigation cost, management remediation, disciplinary response, and enforcement scrutiny.

title: UAE service-recovery and operational-governance profile
sourceTitle: Regional operational-resilience and service-governance guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to service degradation, backlog growth, workflow breakdown, manual fallback strain, and recovery-governance weakness. Emphasis is on recovery effort, service restoration delay, and management escalation cost.

title: UAE workforce welfare and staffing-resilience profile
sourceTitle: Regional worker-welfare, accommodation, and staffing-resilience guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for fatigue, staffing weakness, contractor welfare, and accommodation-control concerns that begin to affect safe delivery and continuity. Emphasis is on remediation, management intervention, and wider legal or confidence effects.

title: U.S. records-of-processing and approved-use profile
sourceTitle: U.S. privacy-program, records-management, and approved-use governance guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for records-of-processing gaps, retention breakdown, approved-use failures, and weak data-accountability controls. Emphasis is on remediation, legal review, and reporting-confidence pressure rather than pure breach response.

title: U.S. supplier dependency and substitute-cost profile
sourceTitle: U.S. supplier concentration and dependency-resilience guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to single-source dependency, substitute-cost escalation, delivery slippage, and upstream resilience weakness. Emphasis is on business interruption, substitute cost, and contract pressure.

title: U.S. procurement diligence and sourcing-integrity profile
sourceTitle: U.S. procurement, due-diligence, and vendor-selection guidance
summary: Directional starting profile aligned to sourcing-governance weakness, poor diligence, vendor-selection failure, and conflicts in the procurement path. Emphasis is on commercial leakage, remediation, and third-party exposure.

title: U.S. contract-rights and indemnity dispute profile
sourceTitle: U.S. contract-governance and dispute-resolution guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for indemnity disputes, licensing conflicts, delivery-rights challenges, and contract remedy pressure. Emphasis is on dispute cost, delay, and commercial exposure.

title: UK conduct, speak-up, and investigation-response profile
sourceTitle: UK conduct, whistleblowing, and internal-investigation enforcement guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for retaliation, hotline failure, gifts-and-hospitality misconduct, and weak investigation follow-through under a UK governance and conduct lens.

title: India outage recovery and service-restoration profile
sourceTitle: India outage, continuity, and service-recovery benchmark profile
summary: Directional starting profile for critical-service disruption, weak failover, restoration delay, and crisis-management strain in India-based operating contexts.

title: APAC privacy operations and data-governance profile
sourceTitle: APAC privacy-programme, retention, and approved-use governance guidance
summary: Directional starting profile for weak records of processing, approved-use drift, retention failure, and privacy-governance gaps across APAC-regulated data estates.

title: North Africa operational service-recovery profile
sourceTitle: North Africa service continuity and recovery benchmark profile
summary: Directional starting profile for backlog growth, degraded service recovery, manual fallback strain, and weak restoration governance in North Africa operating environments.
